results.caseName: NY State CRF — T. Rowe Price termination

metrics.evidence: T. Rowe Price, a global manager within the CRF public equities portfolio, was terminated
The account value at the time of termination was approximately $2 billion
CVC Capital Partners Globetrotter SCSp – CVC Capital Partners – $13.42 million (funded from rollover capital)
Kreos Capital VIII – BlackRock, Inc. – $200 million

metrics.fund: Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II, L.P.

metrics.fund: Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II Co-Investment
metrics.evidence: Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II Co-Investment – Stonepeak Partners L.P. – $80 million
DIF Infrastructure VIII – CVC DIF Management – €250 million
DIF Value-Add IV – CVC DIF Management – €250 million
no external manager activity in the CRF's fixed income portfolio for November 2025
no activity in the CRF's real estate portfolio for November 2025
no activity in the CRF's opportunistic and absolute return strategies portfolio for November 2025
no activity in the CRF's emerging manager portfolio for November 2025

signals.description: T. Rowe Price terminated as global equity manager on November 4, 2025; ~$2B account value reallocated to cash by NY State CRF.
CVC Capital Partners Globetrotter SCSp funded via rollover capital from CVC Asia Pacific Fund IV, L.P., not new capital commitment.
Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II Co-Investment ($80M) invests alongside Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II, L.P. ($243.545M); both closed November 3, 2025.
CVC DIF Management received two simultaneous €250M commitments (DIF Infrastructure VIII and DIF Value-Add IV), both closing November 18, 2025.
All active manager commitments in November 2025 were with existing CRF relationships; no new manager relationships initiated.

1-Year IRR of 8.40% confirmed on both page 4 (highlights) and page 5 (Portfolio Summary table, Year Ended 6/30/2025 column).

signals.description: Target return of 12-14% Net IRR stated on page 3 (IPC summary slide) and corroborated by aggregate ~12% net IRR track record cited on page 1 memo.
Target fund size of $7.5 billion confirmed on both page 3 (summary slide) and page 4 (IPC report vehicle information table).
Total proposed NJ commitment of ~$300M split equally: $150M to ASF IX Infrastructure B, L.P. and $150M to co-investment sidecar; confirmed across pages 1, 3, and 4.
All prior fund performance metrics (ASF VI, VII, VIII) are as of 6/30/2025 per Aksia LLC sourcing note on page 4.
Co-investment sidecar carries 0% management fee and 0% carry vs. the main fund's 1%/12.5% structure, but has an additional 0.2% per annum transaction fee on cash at work.

metadata.source_organization: State of New Jersey, Division of Investment
metadata.document_type: Investment Policy Committee Report / Investment Memorandum

results.caseName: DCRB — noisy board minutes (should flag weak)

metrics.evidence: No IRR/TVPI/DPI data reported in board minutes

signals.description: Board meeting minutes contain no IRR, TVPI, or DPI metrics for private markets. The Investment Committee report references fund commitments (Quantum Energy Partners IX, Updata Fund VIII, Fund AN) but provides no performance metrics. Detailed investment performance data was presented separately at the Investment Committee meeting held earlier the same day, not in these open board minutes.
Total fund net returns reported: CY2025 14.1%, FY2025 10.25%, Fiscal YTD 2026 4.3%, 20-year avg +6.6% p.a. — but these are total fund returns, not private markets IRR/TVPI/DPI.

CIO Patrick Sahm presented a detailed investment update to trustees in a separate morning session (Investment Committee meeting). That presentation likely contains private markets IRR/TVPI/DPI data not included in these open board minutes.

results.rejectReason: Corporate financials detected (7 signals, 0 pension). Likely a manager annual report.
